memory_candidates.content: The /consult legal-analysis-agent uses a four-category output structure for consultation reports: (1) Translation Gaps, (2) Credibility Concerns, (3) Missing Concerns, (4) Strengths to Preserve. The canonical example is at /home/benjamin/Projects/Logos/Vision/specs/203_legal_consult_revisions/reports/01_legal-consult-revisions.md. Each finding uses a **Decision**: [x] Accept  [ ] Reject  [ ] Modify: {notes} checkbox pattern. The Revision Checklist table at the end has columns: # | Item | Lines | Priority | Status ([ ]) and includes only accepted/modified findings.

The legal-analysis-agent.md exists in TWO locations that must stay in sync: (1) /home/benjamin/.config/nvim/.claude/extensions/founder/agents/legal-analysis-agent.md and (2) /home/benjamin/Projects/Logos/Vision/.claude/agents/legal-analysis-agent.md. These files were identical at task 562 research time (2026-05-13). The consult.md command and skill-consult/SKILL.md files also have Vision mirrors but do NOT need changes for the checklist format upgrade.

active_projects.description: Investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills to enable CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing, following corrected understanding of fork mechanics from official Claude Code docs

artifacts.summary: Revised plan to investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills, enabling FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing with 4 phases: audit, pilot, rollout, documentation

Definitive architecture analysis: hybrid inline-agent approach has no precedent, context: fork + orchestration IS proven (slide skills), cache sharing is fundamentally incompatible with named agent routing, recommend rescoping task 500 to documentation-only

memory_candidates.content: All loaded extension skills (neovim-research, neovim-implementation, nix-research, nix-implementation) use the same Pattern A delegation as core skills -- explicit Task tool with subagent_type and multi-stage postflight. They do NOT use context: fork or agent: frontmatter. Only unloaded extension skills (e.g., slide-planning, slide-critic) use Pattern B with context: fork.

The present extension's skill-slide-planning and skill-slide-critic skills use context: fork + agent: in frontmatter AND explicit subagent_type in their Task call. This proves that context: fork with multi-stage orchestration (preflight, interactive Q&A, delegation, postflight) works correctly.
Claude Code agent definitions support a skills field that preloads skill content into the subagent context at startup. This is described as the inverse of context: fork.

active_projects.description: Optimize team-mode skills (team-research, team-plan, team-implement) to maximize FORK_SUBAGENT parallel cache sharing benefits for teammates 2-N

artifacts.summary: Team-mode skills are already eligible for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing; optimization focuses on prompt restructuring, dynamic token multiplier calculation, and cache-aware metadata reporting.
4-phase plan to restructure team-mode prompts for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing, replace hardcoded token multiplier with dynamic formula, and update documentation

memory_candidates.content: TeammateTool spawns in team-mode skills do NOT specify subagent_type, making them eligible for CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing. Teammates 2-N inherit the parent's prompt cache prefix, reducing input token cost by ~90%. Both CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 must be enabled. The actual cache benefit depends on the byte-identical shared prefix ratio -- prompts should put shared content first and teammate-specific assignments last to maximize overlap.

skill-team-research hardcodes team_size=4 in Stage 1 regardless of user-provided --team-size flag. The four fixed roles are: Primary (A), Alternatives (B), Critic (C), Horizons (D). The token_usage_multiplier is hardcoded at 5.0 in all team skill metadata but should be dynamically calculated.

active_projects.description: Research best practices for Claude Code 2026 hook architectures, lifecycle-aware notification systems, and signal-based event patterns for reducing TTS/WezTerm notification spam

artifacts.summary: B+A Hybrid approach: direct invocation from postflight scripts + signal file for Stop hook gating. Evaluates 4 architectures, documents Claude Code 2026 hook event model, WezTerm OSC 1337 limitations.
4-phase B+A Hybrid plan: core signal infrastructure, lifecycle TTS gating, WezTerm multi-state indicators, documentation update
B+A Hybrid lifecycle notification system: 4 phases implemented (tts-notify.sh lifecycle flag, update-task-status.sh integration, WezTerm multi-state indicators, documentation)

memory_candidates.content: Claude Code 2026 Stop hook stdin JSON includes stop_reason, last_assistant_message, agent_id, agent_type, stop_hook_active, and effort fields. The last_assistant_message field contains the full text of Claude's final response and can be pattern-matched for lifecycle detection. The existing memory-nudge.sh hook already demonstrates this pattern successfully.

WezTerm OSC 1337 user variables are write-only from shell scripts (set via escape sequences to pane TTY). They cannot be read back from bash hooks -- only the WezTerm Lua config (format-tab-title, user-var-changed events) can read them via pane:get_user_vars(). This makes OSC 1337 unsuitable as a primary notification gate in hook scripts, though it works well for visual state indicators.

Claude Code 2026 added TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, TeammateIdle, FileChanged, CwdChanged, WorktreeCreate, and WorktreeRemove hook events beyond the original 12. TaskCreated/TaskCompleted require CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and refer to Claude's internal task concept, not custom task management systems.

The B+A Hybrid notification architecture combines direct invocation (postflight calls tts-notify.sh --lifecycle STATUS) with signal file suppression (Stop hook checks specs/tmp/tts-lifecycle-signal before firing TTS). Signal files older than 60 seconds are treated as stale and ignored. Background execution with & prevents blocking the postflight script.

active_projects.completion_summary: Implemented B+A Hybrid lifecycle-aware notification system: tts-notify.sh --lifecycle flag with signal file suppression, update-task-status.sh postflight TTS/WezTerm triggers, wezterm.lua 8-state color-coded tab titles, and comprehensive documentation updates.

active_projects.description: Research optimal model assignments for each agent role in the .claude/ system. Analyze which agents genuinely need Opus-level reasoning vs which could default to Sonnet or inherit. Evaluate implementation agents, research agents, planner, meta-builder, spawn, reviser, and code reviewer roles. Research opusplan mode, model: inherit pattern, effort levels, and CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL integration. Produce a model assignment matrix with cost projections based on 2026 best practices.

artifacts.summary: Model routing research report with complete inventory, pricing analysis, cognitive complexity assessment, model assignment matrix, and cost projections
5-phase implementation plan for tiered model routing with dependency analysis and validation
Implementation summary documenting all tiered model routing changes across the agent system

memory_candidates.content: Claude Code subagent model resolution order: (1) CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL env var, (2) per-invocation model parameter, (3) frontmatter model field, (4) main conversation model. The env var does NOT override agents with explicit model set in frontmatter -- only agents that inherit. Setting model: opus in frontmatter blocks the CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL optimization path.

Anthropic official 2026 model selection matrix explicitly recommends Haiku 4.5 for sub-agent tasks and Sonnet 4.6 for code generation, agentic tool use. Claude Code built-in agents use tiered models: Explore uses Haiku, Plan inherits, statusline-setup uses Sonnet. The opusplan alias uses Opus during plan mode and Sonnet for execution.

2026 Claude model pricing: Opus 4.7 = $5/$25 per MTok (input/output), Sonnet 4.6 = $3/$15, Haiku 4.5 = $1/$5. Opus 4.7 uses a new tokenizer that consumes up to 35% more tokens for the same text. Cache hits are 10% of input price across all models. Sonnet 4.6 scores 79.6% on SWE-bench Verified vs Opus 4.6 80.8% and Opus 4.7 87.6%.

The .claude/ agent system uses a tiered model policy: Opus for deep-reasoning agents (planner-agent, meta-builder-agent, reviser-agent, all formal/lean/math/logic/physics agents, legal-analysis-agent), Sonnet for general-purpose agents (research, implementation, review, spawn, domain-specific agents like neovim/nix/epi/present/latex/founder). Dispatch commands (research, plan, implement) use model: sonnet since the agent model takes precedence. Direct-execution commands keep model: opus. Override flags --opus/--sonnet/--haiku work at invocation time.

active_projects.completion_summary: Implemented tiered model routing: Opus for deep-reasoning agents (planning, formal verification), Sonnet for general-purpose agents (research, implementation, review). Updated 28 agents, 4 commands, and all documentation.

active_projects.description: Add formal escalation protocol and vacuous-definition prohibition to the lean-implementation-agent in /home/benjamin/Projects/ProofChecker/.opencode/. When a phase cannot be completed properly, agent MUST mark it [BLOCKED] with documented blocker and return status partial — never create vacuous Lean definitions (def X := True, def X := Unit) to paper over inability. Also: define vacuous proof explicitly with examples, enforce phase-granular commits (commit after each phase, not batch), add task complexity warning to GATE IN for plans with >20h estimated effort.

active_projects.completion_summary: Added escalation protocol, vacuous-definition prohibition, phase-granular commits, and complexity warning to lean-implementation-agent (both .opencode/ and .claude/extensions/lean/), lean4.md rules, and skill-lean-implementation/SKILL.md

memory_candidates.content: When a lean agent cannot complete a proof phase, it must follow an Escalation Protocol: (1) mark the phase [BLOCKED] in the plan file, (2) document the blocker with what was tried and what is needed, (3) return status 'partial' with requires_user_review: true and blocked_phase field. Never create vacuous definitions (def X := True, theorem X := trivial, etc.) to paper over inability — these are semantically equivalent to sorry and are caught by the Zero-Debt Verification Gate grep pattern.
